We are given the matrix:

$$
A = \begin{bmatrix}
-3 & -4 & -2 & 3 \\
0 & 4 & -4 & -2 \\
3 & -3 & 4 & -2 \\
4 & 2 & -2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
$$

We are to compute the **determinant** of this 4×4 matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use cofactor expansion (along the first row)

The determinant of a 4×4 matrix can be computed using cofactor expansion along the first row:

$$
\det(A) = -3 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
4 & -4 & -2 \\
-3 & 4 & -2 \\
2 & -2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
- (-4) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
0 & -4 & -2 \\
3 & 4 & -2 \\
4 & -2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
+ (-2) \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
0 & 4 & -2 \\
3 & -3 & -2 \\
4 & 2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
- 3 \cdot \det\begin{bmatrix}
0 & 4 & -4 \\
3 & -3 & 4 \\
4 & 2 & -2
\end{bmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute each 3×3 determinant

#### First minor:
$$
M_1 = \det\begin{bmatrix}
4 & -4 & -2 \\
-3 & 4 & -2 \\
2 & -2 & -1
\end{bmatrix}
$$

$$
M_1 = 4 \cdot (4 \cdot -1 - (-2) \cdot -2) - (-4) \cdot (-3 \cdot -1 - (-2) \cdot 2) + (-2) \cdot (-3 \cdot -2 - 4 \cdot 2)
$$

$$
= 4 \cdot (-4 - 4) + 4 \cdot (3 - 4) - 2 \cdot (6 - 8)
$$

$$
= 4 \cdot (-8) + 4 \cdot (-1) - 2 \cdot (-2)
= -32 - 4 +
